author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the same lesson of the Gospel, John ix., on the giving sight to the man that was born blind.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the same lesson of the Gospel, John ix., on the giving sight to the man that was born blind.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard the lesson of the Holy Gospel which we are in the habit of hearing; but it is a good thing to be reminded: good to refresh the memory from the lethargy of forgetfulness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in fact this very old lesson has given us as much pleasure as if it were new.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ gave sight to one blind from his birth; why do we marvel?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ is the Saviour; by an act of mercy He made up that which He had not given in the womb.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when He gave that man no eyes, it was no mistake of His surely; but a delay with a view to a miracle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You are saying, it may be, “Whence knowest thou this?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From Himself I have heard it; He just now said it; we heard it all together.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when His disciples asked Him, and said, “Lord, who did sin, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What answer He made, ye, as I did, heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents, but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo then wherefore it was that He delayed when He gave him no eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not give what He could give, He did not give what He knew He should give, when need was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet do not suppose, Brethren, that this man’s parents had no sin, or that he himself had not, when he was born, contracted original sin, for the remission of which sin infants are baptized unto remission of sins.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that blindness was not because of his parents’ sin, nor because of his own sin; “but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we all when we were born contracted original sin: and yet we were not born blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: However enquire carefully, And we were born blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who was not born blind? blind, that is, in heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord Jesus, for that He had created both, cured both.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With the eyes of faith ye have seen this man blind, ye have seen him too of blind seeing; but ye have heard him erring.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherein this blind man erred, I will tell you; first, in that he thought Christ a prophet, and knew not that He was the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then we have heard an answer of his entirely false; for he said, “We know that God heareth not sinners.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If God heareth not sinners, what hope have we?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If God heareth not sinners, why do we pray, and publish the record of our sin by the beating of the breast?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where again is that Publican, who went up with the Pharisee into the temple and while the Pharisee was boasting, parading his own merits, he standing afar off, and with his eyes fastened on the ground, and beating his breast, was confessing his sins?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this man, who confessed his sins, went down from the temple justified rather than the other Pharisee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Assuredly then God doth hear sinners.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he who spake these words had not yet washed the face of the heart in Siloa.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sacrament had gone before on his eyes; but in the heart had not been yet effected the blessing of the grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When did this blind man wash the face of his heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Lord admitted him into Himself after he had been cast out by the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He found him, and said to him as we have heard; “Dost thou believe on the Son of God?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he, “Who is He, Lord, that I may believe on Him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With the eyes, it is true, he saw already; did he see already in the heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, not yet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wait; he will see presently.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Jesus answered him, “I that speak with thee am He.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did he doubt?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, forthwith he washed his face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he was speaking with That Siloa, “which is by interpretation, Sent.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is the Sent, but Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who often bare witness, saying, “I do the will of My Father That sent Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then was Himself the Siloa.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The man approached blind in heart, he heard, believed, adored; washed the face, saw.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who cast him out continued blind, forasmuch as they cavilled at the Lord, that it was the sabbath when He made clay of the spittle, and anointed the eyes of the blind man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the Lord cured with a word, the Jews openly cavilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He did no work on the sabbath day, when He spake, and it was done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was a manifest cavil; they cavilled at Him merely commanding, they cavilled at Him speaking; as if they did not themselves speak all the sabbath day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I might say that they do not speak not only on the sabbath, but on no day, forasmuch as they have kept back from the praises of the True God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, as I have said, brethren, it was a manifest cavil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord said to a certain man, “Stretch forth thine hand;” he was made whole, and they cavilled for that He healed on the sabbath day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What did He do? what work did He do? what burden did He bear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in this instance, the spitting on the ground, the making clay, and anointing the man’s eyes, is doing some work.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one doubt it, it was doing a work.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord did break the sabbath; but was not therefore guilty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is that I have said, “He brake the sabbath”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He, the Light had come, He was removing the shadows.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the sabbath was enjoined by the Lord God, enjoined by Christ Himself, who was with the Father, when that Law was given; it was enjoined by Him, but in shadow of what was to come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holy day, or of the new moon, or of the sabbath days, which are a shadow of things to come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had now come whose coming these things announced.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why do the shadows delight us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Open your eyes, ye Jews; the Sun is present.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do ye know, ye blind in heart? what know ye?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That this man is not of God, because he thus breaketh the sabbath day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sabbath, unhappy men, this very sabbath did Christ ordain, who ye say is not of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye observe the sabbath in a carnal manner, ye have not the spittle of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this earth of the sabbath look also for the spittle of Christ, and ye will understand that by the sabbath Christ was prophesied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But ye, because ye have not the spittle of Christ in the earth upon your eyes, ye have not come unto Siloa, and have not washed the face, and have continued blind, blind to the good of this blind man, yea now no longer blind either in body or heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He received clay with the spittle, his eyes were anointed, he came to Siloa, he washed his face, he believed on Christ, he saw, he continued not in that exceedingly fearful judgment; “For judgment I came into this world, that they which see not may see, and that they which see may be made blind.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Exceeding alarm!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That they which see not may see:” Good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a Saviour’s office, a profession of healing power, “That they which see not may see.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what, Lord, is that Thou hast added, “That they which see may be made blind”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we understand, it is most true, most righteous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet what is, “They which see”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do they then see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: According to their own words, they see; according to the truth, they do not see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “they see”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They think they see, they believe they see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they believed they did see, when they maintained the Law against Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We know;” therefore they see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “We know,” but we see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “this Man is not of God, because He thus breaketh the sabbath day”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They see; they read what the Law said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it was enjoined that whosoever should break the sabbath day, should be stoned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore said they that He was not of God; but though seeing, they were blind to this, that for judgment He came into the world who is to be the Judge of quick and dead; why came He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That they which see not may see:” that they who confess that they do not see, may be enlightened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And that they which see may be made blind;” that is, that they who confess not their own blindness, may be the more hardened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, in fact, “That they which see may be made blind,” has been fulfilled; the defenders of the Law, Doctors of the Law, the teachers of the Law, the understanders of the Law, crucified the Author of the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O blindness, this is that which “in part hath happened to Israel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Christ might be crucified, and the fulness of the Gentiles might come in, “blindness in part hath happened to Israel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “that they which see not may see”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the fulness of the Gentiles might come in, “blindness in part hath happened to Israel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The whole world lay in blindness; but He came, “that they which see not may see, and that they which see may be made blind.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was disowned by the Jews, He was crucified by the Jews; of His Blood He made an eye-salve for the blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who boasted that they saw the light, being more hardened, being made blind, crucified the Light.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What great blindness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They killed the Light, but the Light Crucified enlightened the blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear one seeing, who once was blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, against what a cross they have miserably stumbled, who would not confess their blindness to the Physician!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Law had continued with them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What serveth the Law without grace?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Unhappy men, what can the Law do without grace?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doeth the earth without the spittle of Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doeth the Law without grace, but make them more guilty?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because hearers of the Law and not doers, and hereby sinners, transgressors.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The son of the hostess of the man of God was dead, and his staff was sent by his servant, and laid upon his face, but he did not revive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What doeth the Law without grace?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What saith the Apostle, now seeing, now of blind, enlightened?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For if there had been a Law given which could give life, verily righteousness should have been by the Law.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take heed; let us answer and say; what is this that he hath said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If there had been a Law given which could give life, verily righteousness should have been by the Law.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it could not give life, why was it given?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He went on and added, “But the Scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by the faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the promise of illumination, the promise of love by the faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe, that Scripture, that is the Law, hath concluded all under sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “hath concluded all under sin”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I had not known concupiscence, except the Law had said, Thou shalt not lust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “hath concluded all under sin”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hath made the sinner a transgressor also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it could not heal the sinner.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “It hath concluded all under sin;” but with what hope?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The hope of grace, the hope of mercy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast received the Law: thou didst wish to keep it, thou wast not able; thou hast fallen from pride, hast seen thy weakness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Run to the Physician, wash the face.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Long for Christ, confess Christ, believe on Christ; the Spirit is added to the letter, and thou wilt be saved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou take away the Spirit from the letter, “the letter killeth;” if it kill, where is hope?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But the Spirit giveth life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then Gehazi, Elisha’s servant, receive the staff, as Moses the servant of God received the Law.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him receive the staff, receive it, run, go before, anticipate him, lay the staff upon the face of the dead child.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so it was; he did receive it, he ran, he laid the staff upon the face of the dead child.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to what purpose? what serveth the staff?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If there had been a Law given which could give life,” the boy might have been raised to life by the staff; but seeing that “the Scripture hath concluded all under sin,” he still lies dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why hath it concluded all under sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That the promise by the faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let then Elisha come, who sent the staff by the servant to prove that he was dead; let him come himself, come in his own person, himself enter into the woman’s house, go up to the child, find him dead, conform himself to the members of the dead child, himself not dead, but living.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this he did; he laid his face upon his face, his eyes upon his eyes, his hands upon his hands, his feet upon his feet, he straitened, he contracted himself, being great, he made himself little.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He contracted himself; so to say, he lessened himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For being in the Form of God, He emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is He conformed Himself, alive to the dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do ye ask, what this is?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle; “God sent His Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, he conformed himself to the dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him tell this, let him go on and declare it again; “In the likeness of flesh of sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is to conform Himself Alive to the dead; to come to us in the likeness of flesh of sin, not in the flesh of sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Man lay dead in a flesh of sin, the likeness of flesh of sin conformed Himself to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He died who had not wherefore to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He died, Alone “Free among the dead;” forasmuch as the whole flesh of men was indeed a flesh of sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how should it rise again, had not He who had no sin, conforming Himself to the dead, come in the likeness of flesh of sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O Lord Jesus, who hast suffered for us, not for Thyself, who hadst no guilt, and didst endure its punishment, that thou mightest dissolve at once the guilt and punishment.
